tagline: Hands-on AI & research courses for high-school students.

courses.title: The AI Toolbox
courses.subtitle: From perception to publication — a 15-class research track.

decks.title: What Is AI? What Can AI Actually Do?
decks.summary: A full 60-min lesson: AI history, traditional vs. current vs. future AI, the research workflow, quizzes, live demos, and two interactive Python labs with an AI assistant.

decks.title: What Is Research? Finding an AI Question
decks.summary: Turn 'I'm interested in X' into a question you could actually study: the research loop, good vs. bad questions, the input→output→metric recipe, SCAMPER, literature search, live demos, and two interactive Python labs.

decks.title: The AI Research Pipeline
decks.summary: See the whole research journey before walking a single step: the Data → Model → Experiment → Conclusion loop, the 5-step professional lifecycle, supervised vs. unsupervised, Colab & Kaggle, and two Python labs — including the full pipeline in 25 lines.

decks.title: ChatGPT for Research
decks.summary: Turn an LLM into a real research assistant — and learn to distrust it productively: prompt anatomy (role · context · format · constraints), the summary/contribution/limitation framework, hallucinations and the 2-minute verify routine, plus a build-your-own tiny language model lab.

decks.title: Choose Your Major — What Universities Like Princeton Offer
decks.summary: A college-planning bridge: tour Princeton's 37 concentrations (A.B. vs B.S.E.) and its Statistics & Machine Learning minor, see how an intended major drives a top-university application, map your interests → major → a research topic that supports it, and get a script for the conversation with your parents.

decks.title: Data Analysis Tools — Python, Gently
decks.summary: Handle real data with a few lines of Python — no fear required: everything-is-a-table, pandas' four verbs, boolean filtering, missing values, the AV detection log, real Titanic analysis in the browser, and your first cleaned dataset + chart in Colab.

decks.title: The Research Toolbox
decks.summary: Assemble the toolkit that turns a curious question into a grounded one: the modern research stack (Elicit, Semantic Scholar, Connected Papers), reading citation graphs, the five-step literature workflow, GitHub for research, and building your own 5-paper literature map.

decks.title: What Is Data? How to Obtain It
decks.summary: Understand where data comes from — and judge it before you trust it: structured vs. unstructured, quantitative vs. qualitative, the three data wells, crawlers & APIs, licenses & collection-story bias, the AV dataset shelf, and your project's dataset datasheet.

decks.title: Data Cleaning & Preprocessing
decks.summary: Turn raw, messy data into something a model can actually learn from: the five enemies of clean data, the three moves (missing · normalize · encode), the one-hot trap, shortcut learning and the X-ray token story, feature engineering, and your own before/after cleaning report.

decks.title: KNN, SVM & a First Neural Network

courses.title: AI Algorithm Patterns
courses.subtitle: The handful of computational patterns that power modern AI — one pattern, one mini-course at a time.

decks.title: The Top-K Pattern: Heaps, Search & Beam Search
decks.summary: An interactive heap-insertion animation, then trace the exact same 'keep only the best K' pattern through KNN, search engines, RAG, beam search, and self-driving cars.

decks.title: Convolution & CNNs: The Sliding Window That Sees
decks.summary: Paint pixels and slide a 3×3 kernel over them live — watch edges light up — then stack the trick into CNNs (face unlock, tumor spotting, self-driving) and meet the RNN, the pattern for sequences.

decks.title: Two Pointers & Sliding Window: How Attention Scans a Sequence
Binary Search: From Sorted Arrays to Hyperparameter Tuning
BFS/DFS: Search & Planning Inside AI Agents

courses.title: Guides · Step-by-Step Notes
courses.subtitle: Interactive tutorial notes for the tools we use in class — read a step, do it, repeat until it sticks.

decks.title: The Terminal, File Paths, VS Code, Git & GitHub, SSH
decks.summary: Step-by-step notes with a built-in practice terminal: pwd/ls/cd, finding a file's path for read_csv, VS Code basics, git init → add → commit → push/pull, branch & merge, and SSH keys — with guided missions, quizzes, and saved progress.

decks.title: Git & the Terminal — The Video Game Edition 🎮
decks.summary: Guide 01 retold entirely in gamer language for younger students: the terminal is a command console, commits are save points, GitHub is cloud saves + multiplayer, branches are creative-mode worlds, SSH keys are keycards — with 5 quests in the built-in practice terminal.

decks.title: Your First Video-Game Research Project 🎮📊
decks.summary: Research methods for a student who loves games: what makes a data-answerable question, an interactive question builder, a sortable slice of the real vgsales dataset with a live genre chart, a catalog of free game datasets (Kaggle, Steam, esports, Twitch), and the 6-step pipeline with pandas starter code.

decks.title: Publish Your Research — a Real Website on GitHub Pages 🌐
decks.summary: Continue from Guide 03 (or start from zero): an interactive research-page builder (fill the form, watch a live preview, download index.html), modeled on a real academic companion site — then two publish paths: clicks-only upload, or the git ritual with a practice-terminal mission. Ends with a live link at USERNAME.github.io/my-research/.

decks.title: Terminal Quest — Launch Night 🕹️
decks.summary: A 6-level command-line adventure game: you are the new engineer at a game studio on launch night. Find the door code with pwd/ls/cd, dig up a lost save file, make git save points, branch a risky fix — then the boss fight: SSH into the crashed game server, repair it with cp, and restart it. Real commands, XP, level map, saved progress.

courses.title: Python, Gently
courses.subtitle: A beginner-friendly path to coding for data & research. (Coming soon.)
